description: if true, a secret object will be generated with auto-generated passwords. This secret object is NOT managed with corresponding releases and NOR part of Helm deployment/upgrade! It's generated once, and if it's deleted, you will lose the secrets.
defines the name of the secret object that has the auto-generated passwords.
(DEPRECATED - use global.license.secret instead) if set, it will be exposed as "CAMUNDA_LICENSE_KEY" in the apps.
(DEPRECATED - use global.license.secret instead) you can provide an existing secret name for Camunda license secret.
(DEPRECATED - use global.license.secret instead) you can provide the key within the existing secret object for Camunda license key.
can be used to provide the license as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the license.
defines the key within the existing secret object.

Adapt the securityContext sections of the deployment to make them compatible with Openshift restricted-v2 SCC: remove runAsUser, runAsGroup and fsGroup and let the platform use their allowed default IDs. Possible values: force (perform the adaptation always), disabled (do not perform adaptation)
if true, enables compatibility layer for Camunda Orchestration Cluster between Camunda 8.7 chart (12.x.x) and Camunda 8.8 chart (13.x.x).
defines applications request body size which used for upload files.
if true, then enable multitenancy in all applicable components.
defines the authentication method which should be used. Possible values: basic, oidc. Could be overridden in each component.
Create config that will be used in Camunda Console.
Name of the application
Can be used to set container image registry.
defines the tag / version which should be used in the most of the apps.
defines the image pull policy which should be used https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#image-pull-policy
can be used to configure image pull secrets https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod
if true, an ingress resource is deployed. Only useful if an ingress controller is available, like Ingress-NGINX.
if true, the Ingress object will not be rendered. This setting is used when a non-Ingress resource, like a service mesh, is used to access the cluster.
Ingress.className defines the class or configuration of ingress which should be used by the controller
If not specified the rules applies to all inbound http traffic, if specified the rule applies to that host. Supports templating (e.g., {{ .Release.Name }}.example.com).
can be used to define the Ingress path type. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/ingress/#path-types
if true, then tls is configured on the ingress resource. If enabled the Ingress.host need to be defined.
defines the secret name which contains the TLS private key and certificate
if true, enables elasticsearch for all components
if true, tries to connect to an external elasticsearch
if true, enable tls for external elasticsearch
(DEPRECATED - use global.elasticsearch.tls.secret instead) can be used to provide an already existing tls secret for connecting to external elasticsearch
can be used to provide the name of an existing Kubernetes secret containing TLS certificate
can be used to provide the key within the secret containing the certificate
the username for external elasticsearch
(DEPRECATED - use global.elasticsearch.auth.secret instead) the password for external elasticsearch
(DEPRECATED - use global.elasticsearch.auth.secret instead) you can provide an existing secret for the external elasticsearch password
(DEPRECATED - use global.elasticsearch.auth.secret instead) you can provide an existing secret key for the external elasticsearch password
can be used to provide the password as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the password.
defines the key within the existing secret object.
(DEPRECATED - use global.elasticsearch.enabled instead) this value is not needed anymore.
defines the elasticsearch access protocol.
Elasticsearch.host defines the elasticsearch host, ideally the service name inside the namespace
Elasticsearch.port defines the elasticsearch port, under which elasticsearch can be accessed
defines the name of the Elasticsearch cluster.
defines the prefix which is used by the old Zeebe Elasticsearch Exporter to create Elasticsearch indexes and consumed by Optimize.
enabled external opensearch
Enabling AWS IRSA
if true, enable tls for external opensearch
(DEPRECATED - use global.opensearch.tls.secret instead) can be used to provide an already existing tls secret for connecting to external opensearch
can be used to provide the name of an existing Kubernetes secret containing TLS certificate
can be used to provide the key within the secret containing the certificate
the username for external opensearch
(DEPRECATED - use global.opensearch.auth.secret instead) the password for external opensearch
(DEPRECATED - use global.opensearch.auth.secret instead) you can provide an existing secret for the external opensearch password
(DEPRECATED - use global.opensearch.auth.secret instead) you can provide an existing secret key for the external opensearch password
can be used to provide the password as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the password.
defines the key within the existing secret object.
defines the external opensearch access protocol
defines the external opensearch host, ideally the service name inside the namespace
defines the external opensearch port, under which opensearch can be accessed
defines the name of the OpenSearch cluster.
defines the prefix which is used by the old Zeebe OpenSearch Exporter to create OpenSearch indexes and consumed by Optimize.
ZeebeClusterName defines the cluster name for the Zeebe cluster. All Zeebe pods get this prefix in their name and the brokers uses that as cluster name.
It's useful for using existing Keycloak in another namespace with and access it with the combined Ingress.
In Keycloak v16.x.x it's hard-coded as '/auth', but in v19.x.x it's '/'.
defines Keycloak realm path used for Camunda.
if true, enables authentication on Management Identity .
defines the issuer name, which is used by the services to validate the JWT tokens.
defines the issuer backend URL, which is used by the services to validate the JWT tokens in a container to container context.
defines the authentication URL, which is used by the services to to obtain authorized access.
defines the token URL, which is used by the services to request JWT tokens.
defines the JWKS URL, which is used by the services to validate the JWT tokens.
defines the type of authentication which should be used. Possible values: `KEYCLOAK`, `MICROSOFT`, or `GENERIC`.
Can be overwritten if ingress is in use and an external IP is available.
if true, creates the admin client which is used in administration operations if needed.
defines the client id.

[string,object] (DEPRECATED - use global.identity.auth.admin.secret instead) A string literal password can be provided. If not set, a random secret is generated. Recommended: reference an existing Kubernetes Secret using global.identity.auth.admin.existingSecret.name.
(DEPRECATED - use global.identity.auth.admin.secret instead) defines the key within the existing secret object.
can be used to provide the client secret as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the client secret.
defines the key within the existing secret object.
defines the client id, which is used by Management Identity in authentication flows.
defines the audience, which is used by Management Identity.

[string,object] (DEPRECATED - use global.identity.auth.identity.secret instead) can be used to reference an existing secret. This should ONLY be used for an external OIDC provider. If not set, a random secret is generated.
(DEPRECATED - use global.identity.auth.identity.secret instead) defines the key within the existing secret object.
can be used to provide the client secret as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the client secret. This should ONLY be used for an external OIDC provider. If not set, a random secret is generated.
defines the key within the existing secret object.
defines the initial claim name, which is used by Management Identity to configure initial mapping rules,
defines the initial claim value, which is used by Management  Identity to configure initial mapping rules.
defines the client id, which is used by Console in authentication flows.
defines the audience which is used by Console's client API.
defines the uri for the well known config which is used by Console (optional).
defines the root URL which is used by Keycloak to access WebModeler.
defines the client id, which is used by WebModeler in authentication flows.
defines the audience which is used by WebModeler's client API.
defines the audience which is used by WebModeler's public API.
defines the root URL which is used by Keycloak to access WebModeler.
defines the audience, which is used by Optimize.
defines the client id, which is used by Optimize in authentication flows.

[string,object] (DEPRECATED - use global.identity.auth.optimize.secret instead) A string literal secret can be provided. If not set, a random secret is generated. Recommended: reference an existing Kubernetes Secret using global.identity.auth.optimize.existingSecret.name.
(DEPRECATED - use global.identity.auth.optimize.secret instead) defines the key within the existing secret object.
can be used to provide the client secret as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the client secret.
defines the key within the existing secret object.
defines the root (or redirect) URL, which is used by Keycloak to access Optimize.
The default document store to use. Valid values: inmemory, aws, and gcp.
Enable AWS document store configuration.
Custom prefix for AWS. Default will generate env vars containing 'storeId' such as DOCUMENT_STORE_AWS_CLASS.
AWS region for the S3 bucket. (example: us-east-1)
Name of the AWS S3 bucket.
(Optional) Path/prefix within the S3 bucket.
(Optional) Time-to-live for documents in the S3 bucket (number in days).
Fully qualified class name for the AWS document store provider.

(DEPRECATED - use global.documentStore.type.aws.accessKeyId.secret and global.documentStore.type.aws.secretAccessKey.secret instead) Reference to an existing Kubernetes secret containing AWS credentials.
(DEPRECATED - use global.documentStore.type.aws.accessKeyId.secret instead) Key within the AWS credentials secret for AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.
(DEPRECATED - use global.documentStore.type.aws.secretAccessKey.secret instead) Key within the AWS credentials secret for AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.
can be used to provide the access key ID as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the access key ID.
defines the key within the existing secret object.
can be used to provide the secret access key as a plain-text value for non-production usage.
can be used to reference an existing Kubernetes Secret containing the secret access key.
defines the key within the existing secret object.
Enable GCP document store configuration.
Custom prefix for GCP. Default will generate env vars containing 'storeId' such as DOCUMENT_STORE_GCP_CLASS.
Name of the GCP bucket.
Fully qualified class name for the GCP document store provider.
(DEPRECATED - use global.documentStore.type.gcp.serviceAccount.secret instead) Reference to an existing Kubernetes secret containing GCP credentials.
(DEPRECATED - use global.documentStore.type.gcp.serviceAccount.secret instead) Key in the GCP credentials secret that contains the service-account JSON.
Mount path for the GCP credentials secret.
The file name for the GCP credentials JSON.

defines the key within the existing secret object.
Enable in-memory document store configuration.
Custom prefix for in-memory. Default will generate env vars containing 'storeId' such as DOCUMENT_STORE_INMEMORY_CLASS.
Fully qualified class name for the in-memory document store provider.
Array of extra objects to deploy with the release.
if true, enables engine-only/no-secondary-storage mode; this disables all secondary-storage-dependent features and components.

if true, Management Identity will seed the first user in Keycloak.

defines the last name of the first user; a name is required to use WebModeler
